description: Issue #205 Cat 6: Fix word grouping and coordinate transforms for rotated text

userStories.title: Fix word grouping for 180 and 270 degree rotated text
userStories.description: Several PDFs have chars at 100% but words at 0% due to incorrect word grouping on rotated text. The current WordExtractor uses spatial sorting (left-to-right, top-to-bottom) which breaks for 180° and 270° rotated content. Implement rotation-aware word grouping that uses per-char TextDirection to determine sort order and adjacency.
acceptanceCriteria: Word grouping respects per-char TextDirection for sort order
No regression on horizontal text word grouping
userStories.notes: Key files: crates/pdfplumber-core/src/words.rs (WordExtractor), crates/pdfplumber-core/src/text.rs (TextDirection). For 180° rotation, chars advance right-to-left and bottom-to-top. For 270° rotation, chars advance bottom-to-top. The WordExtractor should group chars by TextDirection and apply appropriate sort order for each direction. Python pdfplumber handles this via text_direction parameter in word extraction. Check how per-char direction (issue #181) interacts with word grouping.

userStories.title: Fix page-level rotation CTM coordinate transforms
userStories.description: rotated_pages.pdf (benchmark) has F1=0.241 for chars because page-level /Rotate values are not correctly composed into the CTM transformation matrix. The coordinate transform should map rotated PDF coordinates back to the canonical top-left origin coordinate system.
acceptanceCriteria: rotated_pages.pdf (benchmark) chars F1 improves from 0.241 to >=0.80
Page /Rotate values (90, 180, 270) are correctly applied to CTM
Rotated page coordinates match Python pdfplumber golden data
userStories.notes: Key files: crates/pdfplumber-parse/src/interpreter.rs (CTM), crates/pdfplumber/src/page.rs. The /Rotate value in the page dictionary should pre-multiply the initial CTM before interpreting content streams. Rotation angles: 90° = [0 1 -1 0 w 0], 180° = [-1 0 0 -1 w h], 270° = [0 -1 1 0 0 h] where w/h are page dimensions. Compare Rust char coordinates vs Python golden data for rotated_pages.pdf to identify the exact transform error.
